free_text: Angular momentum dependence in 22 MeV alpha-
particle elastic scattering by light nuclei.
Cyclotron was collimated onto a 3 mm*4 mm spot
The scattered alpha-particles were detected by 300 mu-m
silicon junctions located at 8 degr. angular intervals.
The elastic scattering of alpha particles by gold at 40
degr. was used to monitor the beam independently of the
counting rate: the seven detectors and the monitor
signals were mixed at the input of the analog-to-
digital converter (ADC) and routed, by an AP32
Intertechnique logic circuit, to the eight 512-channel
zones of a PDP-9 computer. The monitor was calibrated
by measuring the alpha particle current in a
cylindrical Faraday cup protected against secondary
electrons by a high-voltage ring electrode.
The target characteristics are given in table 1.
